member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.CommonFunction
member.summary: Class for common functions.

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.FileWrapper
member.summary: Wrapper class to wrap System.IO.File methods used in this file to be able to mock them for unit testing.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.FileWrapper.ReadAllText(System.String)
member.summary: Reads all text from a File.

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.IFileWrapper
member.summary: Interface to wrap System.IO.File methods used in this file to be able to mock them for unit testing.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.IFileWrapper.ReadAllText(System.String)
member.summary: Reads all text from a File.

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition
member.summary: Represents test condition handler.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.GetName
member.summary: Gets the name of the test condition that this object represents.
member.returns: Returns the name of the test condition that this object represents.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.GetPinAttributeValue(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Retrieval of a pin attribute value for the particular test condition.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.GetSpecSetValue(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieval of a specification set value for the particular test condition.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.SetDcTestCounterName(System.String)
member.summary: Set the counter name for Dc Testing.
member.param: The standard counter name for DC tests.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.Apply(Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType)
member.summary: Apply the test condition with the given smartTCCategory. If the smartTCCategory is LEVELS_SETUP, and if powerUp is being defined, power up LevelsTc will be applied before the particular test condition being applied. If the smartTCCategory is TIMING, and if the HdmtApi::IsKeepAliveExecuting() return true, skip applying the TimingsTc. If the SmartTC is enabled, it will skip applying if the same test condition name is being applied before under same smartTCCategory type. If the SmartTC is disabled, it will give warning to inform user that the SmartTC is being disabled.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.Apply
member.summary: Apply the test condition with the given smartTCCategory. If the smartTCCategory is LEVELS_SETUP, and if powerUp is being defined, power up LevelsTc will be applied before the particular test condition being applied. If the smartTCCategory is TIMING, and if the HdmtApi::IsKeepAliveExecuting() return true, skip applying the TimingsTc. If the SmartTC is enabled, it will skip applying if the same test condition name is being applied before under same smartTCCategory type. If the SmartTC is disabled, it will give warning to inform user that the SmartTC is being disabled.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.ForceApply(Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType)
member.summary: Force apply test condition with smartTccategory being defined Flush the specific SmartTcCategory if the category is not NONE, and apply the particular test condition only If the smartTCCategory is TIMING, and if the HdmtApi::IsKeepAliveExecuting() return true, skip applying the TimingsTc.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.ForceApply
member.summary: Force apply test condition to hardware. With or without smartTcCategory. TODO add extra behavior documentation for all cases.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.Resolve
member.summary: Resolves the current test condition. this in turn may mean a spec set or UserVar evaluate and then a test condition update.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.SetPinAttributeValue(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Allows direct modification of test condition attribute value for a specified pin.
member.remarks: This function only supports modification of Level and Timing Test Conditions.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestCondition.SetSpecSetValue(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Allows direct modification of a SpecSet variable used by a test condition without knowledge of SpecSet selector.
member.remarks: This will impact any Test Condition that also uses that same SpecSet variable/selector combination.

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType
member.summary: Represents SmartTC category types.

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType.LEVELS_SETUP
member.summary: Level / Instance setup category type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType.LEVELS_POWER_ON
member.summary: Level power-on category type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType.LEVELS_POWER_DOWN
member.summary: Level power-down category type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType.TIMING
member.summary: Timing category type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType.RELAY
member.summary: Relay category type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType.THERMAL
member.summary: Thermal category type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType.NONE
member.summary: NONE

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType
member.summary: Represents test condition types.

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType.TIMING
member.summary: Timing test condition type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType.LEVELS
member.summary: Levels test condition type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType.ALL
member.summary: All test condition types

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType.PREBUILT
member.summary: Pre built test condition type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType.THERMALMEASURE
member.summary: Thermal measure test condition type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType.THERMALCONTROL
member.summary: Thermal control test condition type

member.name: F:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType.TRIGGER
member.summary: Trigger test condition type

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService
member.summary: Service handling all test conditions operations Timing , levels, thermal and responsible for handling SmartTC feature.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.Exists(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the given test condition name exists.
member.param: The name of the test condition to check.
member.returns: True if the test condition name exists, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.SetIpEndSequenceTestCondition(System.String,Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Sets the test condition to apply on the "ApplyEndSequence" for the current IP.
member.param: The name of the test condition name.
The session context of this operation.
member.remarks: This function fails if it is called from the Package scope.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.ApplyEndSequence(Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Applies the TPL file specified EndSequence of the currently loaded test plan to the hardware It will throw error if HdmtApi::IsKeepAliveExecuting() return true when this function is being called.
member.param: The session context of this operation.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.CreateTestConditionFromChangeVariable(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Build new test conditions ahead of time. For shmoo, search and similar test methodologies.
member.remarks: For Shmoo, IpSearch and Search usage, to pre-create test condition for better run time performance.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.SetPowerUpTcName(System.String,Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Set the power up TC name. It must be a valid test condition name. For disabling power up TC, you will need to set it to an empty string.
member.param: The session context of this operation.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.GetPowerUpTcName(Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Get the current power up tc name. If it is empty, means the power up TC is disabled.
member.param: The session context of this operation.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.IsSmartTcEnabled
member.summary: Check if smart test condition is enabled.
member.returns: true if the smartTC is enabled; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.EnableSmartTc
member.summary: Enable smartTC capability.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.DisableSmartTc
member.summary: Disable smartTC capability.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.FlushSmartTcCategory(Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType)
member.summary: Flushes the smart test condition state for a given category if SmartTC is enabled. Will throw an exception if the category given is NONE.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.FlushAllSmartTcCategories
member.summary: Flushes all the smart test condition state for all categories if SmartTC is enabled.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.ResolveAllTestConditions
member.summary: After any test program global or spec set parameter was modified, evaluate to propagate the values through all equations in all dependent spec sets, collection sets and test conditions.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.GetTesterPeriodValue(System.String)
member.summary: Takes user input of Domain name and gets applied Timing TC Period Value..

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.GetTestCondition(System.String,Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Create a new test condition object for a given test condition name. It will throw error if the test condition name is invalid.
member.param: The session context of this operation.
member.returns: TestCondition object for newly created TC.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.GetTestCondition(System.String,Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType,Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Create a new test condition object for a given test condition name. It will throw error if the test condition name is invalid.
member.param: The session context of this operation.
member.returns: TestCondition object for newly created TC.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.ITestConditionService.CreateDirectTestCondition(System.String,Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionType,Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Create a direct test condition for a given test condition name and type.
member.param: Name of the new test condition.
Type of test condition (Levels, Timing, ThermalMeasure, or ThermalControl).
The session context of this operation.
member.returns: TestCondition object for newly created TC.

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.TestCondition
member.summary: TestCondition implementation.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.TestCondition.#ctor(System.String,Prime.TestConditionService.SmartTcCategoryType,Prime.SharedStorageService.Internal.IUnsafeRow{System.String},Prime.SessionService.ISessionContextProviderContainer)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The session context of this operation.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.TestCondition.#ctor
member.summary:
Initializes a new instance of the class. TestCondition has 'ActionAction' members that are initialized in the regular c'tor and holding handle to Hdmt internals. These handles cannot be deserialized from SharedStorage. Also The regular c'tor cannot be called during deserialization to populate the handles because the deserialization occur on different thread and the HdmtApi cannot identify the current DUT thread and fail.
As a WA this c'tor will be used when deserialization occur (only once per AppDomain) and set a flag that the handles require to be populated before being used.

members.member: Pending to remove after adjust c++ TOS3 implementation to use handle member
Pending to remove after adjust c++ TOS3 implementation to use handle member

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionJsonReader
member.summary: TestConditionJsonReader class.

member.name: M:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionJsonReader.Parse``1(System.String)
member.summary: Parsers input file and returns populated object.

member.name: T:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionService
member.summary: TestConditionService implementation.

members.member: Pending to remove after adjust c++ TOS3 implementation to receive smartTc category

member.summary: Gets or sets the configurations.

member.summary: Json test condition map names.

member.name: P:Prime.TestConditionService.TestConditionsTriggerMap.JsonTestConditions.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the Plist Name.

member.summary: Gets or sets the configuration Name.
